title: Why do AI stocks sometimes fall even after strong earnings?
author: Careful_Interest_421
selftext:
One thing that has always seemed counterintuitive to me is how a company can report strong earnings and still see its stock fall afterward.

It makes me wonder how much of the market reaction is actually about the reported numbers versus expectations that were already priced in beforehand. Sometimes it feels like investors are focused less on what happened last quarter and more on whether future growth can keep up with the optimism already built into the stock price.

For those who follow AI-related companies closely, what do you think tends to matter most after earnings: the actual results, forward guidance, valuation concerns, or something else entirely?
